new_terms.context: Refers to Liquid-to-Air and Liquid-to-Liquid cooling heat exchange methods.
The primary water loop in a data center that connects to the TCS.
A cooling device that uses ambient air to cool a fluid, often replacing chillers.
A cooling tower using evaporation to pre-cool air before it passes over a heat exchanger.
Cooling infrastructure located within the IT equipment area (white space) of a data center.
The number of server racks supported by a single Cooling Distribution Unit.
CDU units placed within the server rows rather than at the facility level.
